# Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

The SentinelQA community is committed to providing a harassment-free
experience for everyone, regardless of age, body size, visible or
invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity
and expression, level of experience, education, socio-economic status,
nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, or sexual identity
and orientation.

## Adopted standard

SentinelQA adopts the **Contributor Covenant, version 2.1** as the
authoritative behavior standard for this project. The full text,
including positive behaviors and unacceptable behaviors, is published
upstream and is incorporated here by reference:

- HTML: <https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/>
- Markdown: <https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/code_of_conduct.md>

When the upstream text is updated, SentinelQA continues to follow the
last accepted version unless this file is updated to point at a newer
release.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies within all SentinelQA community spaces,
and also when an individual is officially representing the community
in public spaces. Examples include using an official email address,
posting via an official social media account, or acting as an
appointed representative at an event.

## Enforcement

Reports of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may
be sent privately to the project maintainers at
**`conduct@sentinelqa.dev`** (owner-provisioned inbox; placeholder
until the public flip). For security issues — vulnerabilities or
abuses of the safety boundary in our engineering rules — use the
private-disclosure path in [`SECURITY.md`](../SECURITY.md), not this
inbox.

All complaints will be reviewed and investigated promptly and fairly.
All community leaders are obligated to respect the privacy and
security of the reporter.

## Enforcement guidelines

Community leaders follow the Contributor Covenant 2.1
**Community Impact Guidelines** to determine the consequences for any
action they deem in violation of this Code of Conduct
(Correction → Warning → Temporary Ban → Permanent Ban).

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct adopts the Contributor Covenant, version 2.1,
available at the URLs above. The Contributor Covenant is itself
distributed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.
